title: Sinners: Ryan Coogler Wins Oscar for Best Original Screenplay
text: Director and screenwriter Ryan Coogler claimed the Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay at the 98th Oscars ceremony on March 15, 2026, for his vampire film "Sinners," marking his first Oscar win. The Achievement: Coogler brought the screenplay to studios roughly two years prior as part of a package deal that included his direction and Michael B. Jordan's starring role. The project ultimately landed at Warner Bros. and has become a major awards contender, entering the ceremony with 16 nominations—the most in Oscar history. His Acceptance Speech: Taking the stage to a standing ovation, Coogler expressed nervousness about his remarks. He stated: "I'm very nervous and they're gonna play me off. I grew up in Oakland, California, and we can talk a lot." The filmmaker thanked the Academy, fellow nominees, and everyone involved in the production. He also expressed gratitude to his wife Zinzi Evans, calling her "the best wife and mom in the world," and acknowledged his parents for instilling self-belief in him. Career Context: Before this triumph, Coogler had already established himself as an accomplished screenwriter, having co-written "Creed" and the "Black Panther" films. "Sinners" represents his first solo screenplay effort since "Fruitvale," the Sundance drama that launched both his and Jordan's feature film careers.
